action: Enforce Builder worktree isolation at kernel layer. Recurring breach pattern (cycles 6, 40, 41) — Builder writes to main instead of per-cycle worktree, causing ship.sh to refuse commit and leaving dirty files on main. Retrospective layer has captured 3 lessons but no enforcement landed. Proposed: add phase-gate check in gate_build_to_audit (scripts/lifecycle/phase-gate.sh) that runs 'git -C $EVOLVE_PROJECT_ROOT diff --quiet HEAD' BEFORE allowing build-to-audit transition; if non-empty, fail-fast with INTEGRITY-BREACH (DISPATCH_RC=2) instead of silent ship-refusal. Auditor profile should also assert active_worktree path is the cycle-N worktree, not main.
evidence_pointer: https://github.com/mickeyyaya/evolve-loop/commit/2522dbc — cycle 41 salvage commit message references the 3 breach incidents and retrospective entries
operator_note: Discovered during $100 autoresearch batch (May 2026). Cycle 41 attempt 1 caused dispatcher to abort retry-then-die when ship.sh detected the breach. Auditor verdict was WARN with all 5 ACs PASS — the work is correct, but the place where it landed is wrong. Lessons in .agents/instincts/lessons/ capture this but kernel enforcement is missing.
